About: It is about private individuals who join an armed conflict for personal profit.
It is about individuals who are outsiders to a conflict and not members of any official military.
It is about individuals who fight for money or other forms of payment rather than for political interests.
It is about the legal protections afforded to combatants in armed conflicts.
It is about the definition and legal status of individuals participating in armed conflicts for payment.
It is about international and national laws of war concerning individuals in armed conflicts.
It is about the United Nations Convention against the recruitment, use, financing and training of mercenaries.
It is about national laws in Austria, France, Germany, South Africa, and the United Kingdom concerning mercenary activities.
It is about foreign national servicemen serving in another country's armed forces.
It is about private military companies providing armed combat or security services for financial gain.
Affects: It affects the legal protections afforded to individuals in armed conflicts.
It affects the interpretation and application of international laws of war.
It affects the citizenship status of individuals in countries like Austria and Germany.
It affects the legality of private military companies and their operations.
It affects the classification of combatants under the Geneva Conventions.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with armed conflict.
It is associated with personal profit.
It is associated with the Geneva Conventions.
It is associated with Protocol Additional to the Geneva Conventions.
It is associated with the United Nations Mercenary Convention.
It is associated with private military companies.
It is associated with foreign enlistment acts.
It is associated with the concept of combatants and prisoners of war.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the domain of international law and armed conflict.
It belongs to the category of individuals participating in conflicts for payment.
It belongs to the discussion of war crimes and protections under the rules of war.
CausedBy: It is caused by the desire for personal profit.
It is caused by the promise or payment of material compensation.
ConsistsOf: It consists of private individuals joining an armed conflict for personal profit.
It consists of individuals who are outsiders to the conflict.
It consists of individuals not members of any official military.
It consists of individuals motivated by financial gain.
It consists of individuals fighting for money or other forms of payment.
HasA: It has a definition provided by Article 47 of Protocol I of the Geneva Conventions.
It has a definition provided by Article 1 of the UN Mercenary Convention.
It has been subject to national laws in various countries, including France, Germany, South Africa, and the United Kingdom.
